positioning.tagline: Online Fitness Coach | Results-driven training with AI-powered accountability
target_audience: busy professionals wanting to get fit
people who've tried and failed before
positioning.differentiation: Tech-savvy coach with real systems - not just another guy with a clipboard
positioning.voice: Knowledgeable, relatable, confident without being arrogant. Speaks to the person who's tried stuff before and failed.
positioning.goal: Drive DMs and client inquiries - not vanity followers

process: 2. When they post, add genuinely helpful comments

4. Profile visitors see you're a coach

quick_tips:
Simple tip that changed everything for my clients:

Eat protein first at every meal.

Why it works: You fill up on what matters, naturally eat less junk, and recovery improves.

Try it this week. DM me how it goes.

Not seeing results?

Here's the fix: Track what you actually eat for 3 days. Not to restrict - just to see reality.

90% of people are shocked. That awareness alone changes behavior.

Takes 5 minutes. Works every time.

Stop chasing motivation.

Start building systems.

The difference: Motivation fades. Systems run automatically.

Simple wins.

The #1 reason people don't see results:

Inconsistency.

Not bad genetics. Not slow metabolism. Not lack of time.

You don't need a perfect plan. You need a plan you'll actually follow.

DM if you need help building one.

3 things that matter more than your workout:

1. Sleep (recovery happens here)
2. Protein (muscle needs building blocks)
3. Consistency (showing up beats intensity)

Nail these first. Everything else is optimization.
myth_busters:
"You need to eat 6 meals a day to boost metabolism."

This is wrong. Here's why:

Meal frequency doesn't affect metabolism. Total calories and protein do.

What actually works: Eat in a pattern that fits YOUR life.

Save this.

Fitness industry lies:

❌ "No pain no gain"
❌ "Carbs make you fat"
❌ "You need supplements to build muscle"

The truth:

✅ Progressive overload, not pain
✅ Excess calories make you fat
✅ Food first, supplements are extras

Stop overcomplicating it.

"You can't build muscle and lose fat at the same time."

Wrong.

If you're a beginner or returning after a break, you absolutely can.

How: High protein + strength training + slight calorie deficit.

Don't let bro science limit you.
relatability:
I used to think I needed the perfect program.

Spent years researching instead of doing.

Then I realized: consistency with a mediocre plan beats inconsistency with a perfect one.

Now I help others avoid the same mistake.

What's your biggest fitness struggle? 👇

Real talk:

Some days you won't want to train. That's normal.

The difference between people who get results and people who don't?

They show up anyway. Even if it's just 20 minutes.

Nobody tells you this, but discipline feels like freedom eventually.

I was a competitive swimmer (Junior Olympics, butterfly).

Then I got into lifting, running, BMX.

Then life happened. Brain cancer at [age]. Still graduated engineering with a 3.8.

Point is: I've been through it. I know what it takes to rebuild.

If you're struggling, I get it. DM me.
client_results:
Client win:

8 weeks → Down 12 lbs, strength up on all lifts

What changed:
• Stopped random workouts, followed a program
• Actually tracked protein (wasn't eating enough)
• Built a morning routine that stuck

No magic. Just consistency + the right plan.

DM 'PROGRAM' if you want results like this.

"I've tried everything and nothing works."

That's what he said in our first call.

12 weeks later:
- 20 lbs down
- First pull-up ever
- Actually looks forward to training

The secret? We found what works for HIS life.

DM me if you're ready to stop trying and start doing.

Client text this morning:

"Just hit a 225 squat. Never thought I'd get here."

6 months ago he could barely do bodyweight.

This is why I coach. These messages.

Want to be my next success story? DM 'START'

profile_optimization.bio_template: Online Fitness Coach | Helping [target] get [result] | Junior Olympic Swimmer → Engineer → Coach | DM 'START' for free consult
bio_examples: Online Fitness Coach | Helping busy pros get lean without living at the gym | DM 'START' for free consult
Results-driven coaching for people who've tried everything | Engineering brain + athlete experience | DM 'FREE' for consult

accounts_to_engage: Large fitness accounts (100K+) in your niche
Accounts whose audience matches your ideal client
comment_types: Answer someone's question in the comments (be helpful, not salesy)
Add a useful insight the original post missed

step_1.response: Hey! Thanks for reaching out. Quick question - what's your main fitness goal right now?

step_2.response: Got it. And what have you tried so far? What's worked, what hasn't?

step_3.response: Makes sense. I think I can help. Want to jump on a quick 15-min call? I'll show you what I'd do differently. No pressure either way. [Calendly link]

step_4.action: Present offer, handle objections, close or follow up

dm_to_client_flow.notes: Keep it conversational. Don't be robotic. The goal is to qualify AND build rapport.

posting_schedule.priority: Quality > Quantity. One great post beats 5 mediocre ones.
posting_schedule.notes: Commenting is MORE important than posting for client acquisition

metrics_to_track.notes: Focus on DMs and calls, not followers. Followers don't pay bills.

if_paid_ads_later.note: Only consider after organic is working. Ads amplify what works, they don't fix what's broken.
